<<Course Structure and How to Get the Most Out of It>>

*highlighted section*  
**1. Understand the Course Layout**  
A well-structured course typically includes modules, lessons, assignments, and assessments. Start by reviewing the syllabus to identify key topics and deadlines. [course name] is designed to build progressively, so [module 1] lays the foundation for [module 2]. Knowing the structure helps you plan your time effectively and avoid last-minute stress.

*highlighted section*  
**2. Prioritize Active Learning**  
Don’t just passively read or watch lectures. Engage with the material by taking notes, asking questions, and participating in discussions. For example, [assignment 1] requires you to apply concepts from [lesson 3], so make sure you understand the core ideas before moving on. [resource 1] provides additional support if you need clarification.

*highlighted section*  
**3. Manage Your Time Wisely**  
Break down larger tasks into smaller, manageable steps. Use a planner or digital tool to track your progress. [due date 1] is a critical milestone, so set reminders and allocate sufficient time for [project 1]. Consistency is key—dedicate [study time per day] to the course to maintain momentum.
